// Technical documentation excerpts with trigger-adjacent words in benign contexts
// Each line is one independent sample

Follow these instructions to install the package: run npm install palisade, then configure your environment variables in a local .env file.
The health endpoint returns server status. Call GET /api/health to check if all services are operational and the database connection is active.
User roles determine access levels. Available roles are admin, editor, and viewer. Assign roles through the dashboard settings page.
This version includes breaking changes from the previous release. See the migration guide for detailed steps on updating your configuration files.
This library provides utilities for prompt template management. Templates support variable interpolation and can be loaded from external files.
Add patterns to your .gitignore file to exclude build artifacts from version control. Common entries include node_modules/, dist/, and .env files.
If the server fails to start, check that all environment variables are configured correctly. Run the diagnostic command to view current settings.
The decodeURI() function accepts a URI-encoded string and returns the original UTF-8 content. It throws URIError if the input contains malformed percent sequences.
Configuration files support multiple environments: development, staging, and production. Each environment can override default values using a dedicated config block.
The logging subsystem writes structured JSON entries to the configured output. Set the level to debug during development and info or warn in production.
